stem: An engineer takes 20 measurements of fuel level using a fuel gauge. The measurements have a mean of 179.3 % and a standard deviation of 14.5 %. However, calibration tests reveal that a fuel gauge systematically overestimates the true value by between 1.5% and 6.3%. Estimate the true fuel level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 179.3 %. After correcting for the 3.9% bias: ≈ 172.6 %. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (1.5%-6.3%) and sampling variability: [162.2, 183.2]. Point estimate: 172.6 %.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
